days.title: Project Gamma Kickoff: The DevOps Mindset

tasks.title: Introduction to Cloud Transformation
tasks.description: Understanding the Project Gamma mission: Moving from 'Click-Ops' to Automation.
tasks.reading_content_md:
# Welcome to Project Gamma

## The Mission
We are transforming a legacy on-premise environment into a modern **Cloud-Native** platform. As part of the 29-member transformation team, your role is to ensure the infrastructure is scalable, secure, and fully automated.

## Core Pillars
1. **Infrastructure as Code (IaC)**: No more manual server setup.
2. **CI/CD**: Automating the path from code commit to production.
3. **Observability**: If it moves, we monitor it.

tasks.title: DevOps Principles Quiz
tasks.description: Verify your understanding of DevOps and Cloud goals.

quiz_questions.question: What does the term 'Infrastructure as Code' mean?
options: Managing and provisioning infrastructure through machine-readable definition files

quiz_questions.question: Which of these is a core pillar of Project Gamma?

tasks.title: How Data Moves

tasks.reading_content_md:
# Network Architecture

To build a cloud, you must understand the network. We focus on **Layer 3 (Network)** for IP routing and **Layer 4 (Transport)** for TCP/UDP ports. In Project Gamma, we will design Virtual Private Clouds (VPCs) that mimic these physical networking concepts.

quiz_questions.question: Which protocol is 'connection-oriented' and ensures data delivery?
What is the standard port for HTTPS traffic?

tasks.description: How AWS organizes data centers for high availability and disaster recovery.
tasks.reading_content_md:
# AWS Foundation

Project Gamma will be hosted on **AWS**. We must design our infrastructure to span multiple **Availability Zones (AZs)**. If one data center goes dark, our automated failover ensures the platform stays online.

quiz_questions.question: An Availability Zone is typically...
options: A collection of one or more physical data centers

tasks.reading_content_md:
# Private vs Public

In Project Gamma, our databases are placed in **Private Subnets**. They have no public IP address. Only our web servers (in Public Subnets) can talk to them, drastically reducing our attack surface.

Project Gamma uses S3 as our 'Data Lake' for logs and application backups. We use **Lifecycle Policies** to automatically move old logs to cheaper storage classes like **Glacier** after 90 days.

tasks.reading_content_md:
# Scaling Project Gamma

We never want a server to be a single point of failure. By using an **ASG**, if an EC2 instance crashes, AWS automatically detects it and launches a new one to maintain our 'Desired Capacity'.

options: The ASG terminates it and launches a replacement

In a 29-person team, we must log every API call. **CloudTrail** records if an engineer deletes an S3 bucket or changes a Security Group, providing the accountability required for enterprise DevOps.

tasks.reading_content_md:
# Month 1 Complete

We have established the **Cloud Foundation**. We have a secure network, a managed database, and automated server scaling. Month 2 will focus on **Infrastructure as Code (Terraform)** to automate everything we did manually this month.

tasks.title: Month 1 Final Quiz
tasks.description: The final assessment for the Month 1 Foundation phase.

quiz_questions.question: Which tool will we introduce in Month 2 to automate infrastructure provisioning?
What is the primary benefit of a NAT Gateway?
options: Allows private instances to access the internet securely

tasks.title: Why Declarative Infrastructure?
tasks.description: Understanding the shift from manual setup to version-controlled infrastructure.
tasks.reading_content_md:
# Month 2: Automation

## The Problem with Manual Config
In Month 1, we used the AWS Console. However, if we need to build 10 identical environments for our developers, doing it manually leads to 'Configuration Drift'.

## The Terraform Solution
Terraform allows us to define our AWS resources in configuration files. 
- **Declarative**: We describe the 'End State' (e.g., 'I want 3 EC2 instances'), and Terraform handles the creation.
- **Idempotent**: Running the same code multiple times results in the same state.

quiz_questions.question: What is 'Configuration Drift'?
options: When manual changes cause environments to become different over time
A feature of the Linux kernel

tasks.reading_content_md:
# Modular Infrastructure

Instead of writing 500 lines of code in one file, we create **Modules**. One team member builds the 'Standard VPC' module, and everyone else 'calls' it with their specific CIDR. This ensures every network in Project Gamma follows the same security standards.

# TODO 2: Set the source to a local folder './modules/vpc'
# TODO 3: Pass a variable 'vpc_name'

tasks.reading_content_md:
# The Power of Ansible

With one command, `ansible-playbook site.yml`, we can update the configuration of 100 servers simultaneously. In Project Gamma, this is how we ensure all web servers have the same security patches and Nginx configs.

# setup.yml
- hosts: all
  become: yes
  tasks:
    # TODO 1: Use 'apt' module to install 'git'
    # TODO 2: Use 'apt' module to install 'curl'

In a dynamic cloud environment, servers are launched and terminated daily. We use **Dynamic Inventory** to tell Ansible: 'Find all servers with the tag Project: Gamma and configure them'.

tasks.title: VMs vs. Containers

tasks.reading_content_md:
# Month 3: Containerization

## Why Docker?
Virtual Machines (VMs) are heavy; each includes a full OS. Containers share the host kernel, allowing us to run dozens of services (like our Java and Python apps) on a single server, saving significant cloud costs.

days.title: Mastering Helm: The K8s Package Manager

tasks.title: Why Helm?
tasks.description: Understanding how to template K8s YAML files to support multiple environments (Dev/Prod).
tasks.reading_content_md:
# Month 4: Orchestration at Scale

## The Template Challenge
In Month 3, we wrote static YAML files. But in Project Gamma, we need different CPU limits and replica counts for Dev vs. Production. 

## Helm Charts
Helm allows us to use **Charts**. We write one template and use a `values.yaml` file to inject the specific settings for each environment, preventing manual errors across our 29-person engineering team.

tasks.title: Helm Basics Quiz
tasks.description: Test your knowledge of Helm components.

quiz_questions.question: Which file in a Helm chart contains the default configuration values?

tasks.title: Charting the Solaris App
tasks.description: Structuring a chart directory and using the 'helm install' command.

tasks.title: Helm Coding Task
tasks.description: Write a snippet of a Helm template using the {{ .Values.replicaCount }} placeholder.
tasks.coding_starter_code:
# templates/deployment.yaml snippet
spec:
  # TODO: Use the double-curly-brace syntax to pull 'replicaCount' from values.yaml
  replicas:

tasks.title: Routing External Traffic
tasks.description: Using NGINX Ingress to route traffic from gamma.com to specific internal K8s services.

tasks.title: Ingress Quiz
tasks.description: Check your understanding of L7 load balancing in K8s.

quiz_questions.question: What is the primary advantage of Ingress over a LoadBalancer service?
options: It allows routing multiple domains/paths through a single IP address

tasks.title: Automating HTTPS
tasks.description: Using Let's Encrypt and cert-manager to automatically issue and renew SSL certificates for our K8s Ingress.
tasks.reading_content_md:
# Secure by Default

Manual certificate management is a security risk. In Project Gamma, we deploy **cert-manager**. When an engineer creates a new Ingress, cert-manager automatically talks to Let's Encrypt and configures the HTTPS certificate without human intervention.

tasks.title: Cert-Manager Task
tasks.description: Write a K8s Ingress annotation that triggers cert-manager to issue a certificate.
tasks.coding_starter_code:
# ingress.yaml metadata snippet
annotations:
  # TODO: Add the cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer annotation

tasks.title: Scaling Based on Traffic
tasks.description: Automatically increasing the number of Solaris app pods when CPU usage spikes.
tasks.reading_content_md:
# Self-Scaling Infrastructure

In Project Gamma, we don't manually add pods. We set a target: 'Keep CPU at 60%'. If traffic increases, the **HPA** tells the deployment to scale from 2 to 10 pods automatically.

tasks.description: Write a 'kubectl' command to autoscale a deployment between 2 and 5 replicas.

# TODO: Use 'kubectl autoscale deployment' with --min and --max flags

tasks.title: Syncing Git to the Cluster
tasks.description: Introduction to GitOps: Making Git the single source of truth for your cluster state.
tasks.reading_content_md:
# The GitOps Way

In Project Gamma, no one runs `kubectl apply` manually in Production. Instead, we use **ArgoCD**. It watches our Git repo and ensures the cluster always matches the code. If someone changes something manually, ArgoCD 'corrects' it back to the Git version.

tasks.title: GitOps Quiz
tasks.description: Check your understanding of the GitOps workflow.

quiz_questions.question: In GitOps, what is the single source of truth?

tasks.title: Production Grade Clusters
tasks.description: How Amazon EKS manages the K8s control plane and high availability for us.

tasks.reading_content_md:

We have reached 'Cloud Native' status. Our applications are auto-scaling, auto-healing, and securely managed via RBAC. Next month: **CI/CD Automation** to connect the developer's code to this cluster.

What is the role of an Ingress Controller?

tasks.title: The Path to Production
tasks.description: Understanding the 'Continuous' philosophy and why manual deployments are forbidden in Project Gamma.
tasks.reading_content_md:
# Month 5: Automation

## The Pipeline Goal
When a developer pushes code to Git, we want to automatically:
1. Run unit tests.
2. Scan for security vulnerabilities.
3. Build a Docker image.
4. Deploy to a staging environment.
This ensures that only 'green' code ever reaches our users.

tasks.title: CI/CD Concepts Quiz
tasks.description: Test your knowledge of pipeline stages.

quiz_questions.question: What is the primary difference between Continuous Delivery and Continuous Deployment?
options: Continuous Deployment automates the release to production without manual intervention

tasks.title: Jenkins Architecture
tasks.description: Setting up Jenkins and understanding the Master-Agent relationship for scaling builds.

tasks.description: Write a command to run Jenkins in a Docker container with persistent storage.
tasks.coding_starter_code:
# run_jenkins.sh
# TODO: Run 'docker run' with port 8080 mapping and a volume for /var/jenkins_home

tasks.title: Declarative vs. Scripted Pipelines
tasks.description: Learning Groovy syntax to define your pipeline stages inside a text file in Git.

tasks.title: Jenkinsfile Quiz
tasks.description: Check your understanding of Jenkins pipeline structure.

quiz_questions.question: In a Declarative pipeline, which block contains the actual work instructions?

tasks.title: Automated Testing in the Pipeline
tasks.description: Integrating JUnit and PyTest into Jenkins to stop the build on failure.

tasks.title: Test Stage Task
tasks.description: Write a Jenkinsfile 'stage' that executes 'mvn test'.

tasks.title: Automated Image Building
tasks.description: Configuring Jenkins to build a Docker image and push it to AWS ECR automatically on successful tests.
tasks.reading_content_md:
# Building the Artifact

In Project Gamma, the Docker image is our 'Artifact'. The pipeline builds the image, tags it with the Git Commit ID, and stores it in ECR. This provides a clear 'Lineage' of which code version is inside which container.

tasks.title: Docker-Jenkins Quiz
tasks.description: Check your knowledge of artifact management.

quiz_questions.question: Why do we tag images with Git Commit IDs in the pipeline?
options: To easily map a running container back to the exact code version in Git

tasks.description: Learning how to use GitHub's built-in automation for modern web projects.

  # TODO: Define the 'push' trigger for 'main'
jobs:
  build:
    # TODO: Set runs-on to ubuntu-latest

tasks.title: Finding Security Flaws Early
tasks.description: Integrating SonarQube or Snyk into the pipeline to find vulnerabilities in code before it's built.

quiz_questions.question: What does 'Shift Left' mean in DevSecOps?

Adding more servers to the left

tasks.title: The Final Stage: CD
tasks.description: Using Jenkins to run 'helm upgrade' and deploy the new image to our EKS cluster.
tasks.reading_content_md:
# Closing the Loop

Once the image is in ECR and the security scan is green, Jenkins uses **Helm** to update the Kubernetes deployment. It updates the 'image tag' to the new version, and K8s performs a rolling update to the 10,000 users.

tasks.description: Write a shell command to be used in Jenkins that updates a Helm release with a new image tag.

quiz_questions.question: Which Terraform command is most important to run in a PR review stage?

tasks.title: The Automated Enterprise
tasks.description: Reviewing the full pipeline: from Git push to a running K8s pod.
tasks.reading_content_md:
# Automation Milestone

We have replaced manual steps with a high-speed automation engine. The 29-member team can now deploy changes with confidence. Next month: **Observability & Launch** to ensure we can monitor these deployments in real-time.

days.title: Introduction to Observability: Logs, Metrics, Traces

tasks.title: The Three Pillars of Observability
tasks.description: Understanding the fundamental data points needed to monitor a complex cloud environment.
tasks.reading_content_md:
# The Observability Mindset

Monitoring tells you *if* a system is down; **Observability** tells you *why*. In a microservices cluster like Project Gamma, we need:
1. **Logs**: Detailed records of events.
2. **Metrics**: Numerical indicators of health (CPU, RAM, Request Rate).
3. **Traces**: The path a single request takes across all services.

tasks.title: Observability Quiz
tasks.description: Test your knowledge of monitoring pillars.

quiz_questions.question: Which pillar is best for identifying trends like 'slowly increasing memory usage'?

tasks.title: Centralizing the Noise
tasks.description: Introduction to Elasticsearch, Logstash, and Kibana (ELK) for searching logs across the cluster.

tasks.title: ELK Logic Task
tasks.description: Write an Elasticsearch query to find all 'ERROR' logs in the last 15 minutes.

tasks.title: Scraping the Cluster
tasks.description: How Prometheus 'scrapes' numerical data from pods and stores it as time-series data.

tasks.title: Prometheus Quiz
tasks.description: Check your understanding of the pull-based monitoring model.

quiz_questions.question: What does Prometheus use to discover which pods to monitor?

tasks.title: Building the Command Center
tasks.description: Connecting Grafana to Prometheus to build real-time dashboards for the 29-member team.
tasks.reading_content_md:
# The Executive Dashboard

In Project Gamma, we don't look at CLI logs all day. We use **Grafana**. It provides a 'Single Pane of Glass' where the **Software Engineers** and **Stakeholders** can see exactly how many users are on the platform and if the response times are within the SLA.

# cpu_usage.promql
# TODO: Use avg(node_cpu_seconds_total) and filter for 'idle' mode

quiz_questions.question: What is a 'Span' in distributed tracing?
options: A single unit of work within a trace (e.g., one API call)

tasks.title: Intelligent Notifications
tasks.description: Configuring rules to notify the team via Slack/PagerDuty *only* when critical thresholds are reached.

tasks.title: Alerting Logic Task
tasks.description: Write a Prometheus alerting rule that triggers if 'Disk Space' is below 10%.
tasks.coding_starter_code:
# alert.yaml
- alert: LowDiskSpace
  expr: # TODO: node_filesystem_avail_bytes / node_filesystem_size_bytes < 0.1
  for: 5m
  labels:
    severity: critical

tasks.title: Proactive Health Checks
tasks.description: Setting up external 'pings' to our production URL to ensure the Ingress and Load Balancers are working.
tasks.reading_content_md:
# The User Perspective

Everything might look 'Green' inside the cluster, but the internet could be blocked. We use **Synthetic Monitoring** to periodically send requests from different global regions (US, India, Europe) to verify our actual end-user experience.

tasks.title: Synthetic Quiz
tasks.description: Check your understanding of external monitoring.

quiz_questions.question: What is the primary goal of Synthetic Monitoring?
options: To check if the database is full
To simulate user behavior and verify uptime from outside the network
To count the number of developers

tasks.title: Lessons Learned
tasks.description: A deep dive into the 6-month journey: what went well and what we would do differently.
tasks.reading_content_md:
# Transformation Complete

Project Gamma has moved from manual scripts to a global, automated, and observable cloud platform. The 29-member team has mastered Linux, AWS, Terraform, Docker, and K8s. We are now ready to maintain and scale this platform for the future.

tasks.title: Final Milestone Quiz
tasks.description: The ultimate final exam for the Project Gamma Cloud & DevOps curriculum.

quiz_questions.question: Which tool was used as the source of truth for our Infrastructure?
What is the primary purpose of the 'Control Plane' in Kubernetes?
options: To manage the cluster's state and schedule workloads

tasks.title: The Next Phase
tasks.description: Official project closure and defining the roadmap for Phase 2: Multi-cloud and Serverless.

tasks.title: Handover Task
tasks.description: Create a final 'Project Gamma Summary' JSON object for the handover documentation.

tasks.title: Structured Logging for Systems
tasks.description: Moving beyond plain text logs to JSON format for easier parsing and searching.

tasks.title: Logging Quiz
tasks.description: Test your knowledge of log management.

quiz_questions.question: Why is JSON preferred over plain text for enterprise logs?
options: It is easily searchable by machines and tools like Elasticsearch

tasks.title: The Data Transformer
tasks.description: Learning how Logstash filters, parses, and enriches logs before sending them to Elasticsearch.

tasks.title: Logstash Task
tasks.description: Write a basic Logstash 'grok' filter to parse a standard Nginx access log line.

tasks.title: Visualizing Latency and Error Rates
tasks.description: Building the 'Golden Signals' dashboard: Latency, Traffic, Errors, and Saturation.

tasks.title: Dashboard Quiz
tasks.description: Check your understanding of the Golden Signals.

quiz_questions.question: Which Golden Signal tracks how full your server's CPU or Memory is?

tasks.title: Signal vs. Noise
tasks.description: Designing alert thresholds that only wake up engineers for actionable, high-priority issues.
tasks.reading_content_md:
# Preventing Burnout

If we send a Slack alert for every minor CPU spike, the team will start to ignore them. In Project Gamma, we only alert on **Symptoms** (e.g., users getting 500 errors) rather than **Causes** (e.g., high CPU), unless it's critical.

tasks.title: Alerting Task
tasks.description: Write a Slack notification template for a 'System Down' alert.
tasks.coding_starter_code:
ALERT: *{{ $labels.alertname }}*
# TODO: Include the Severity, Environment, and Summary variables

tasks.title: Simulating the User Journey
tasks.description: Setting up automated scripts that try to 'log in' and 'view projects' every 5 minutes from the outside world.

tasks.title: Uptime Quiz
tasks.description: Check your knowledge of synthetic monitoring.

quiz_questions.question: What is the primary benefit of Synthetic monitoring?
options: It alerts us if the site is unreachable for users before we hear it from them

tasks.title: The Final Quality Gate
tasks.description: Conducting a formal review of the entire system architecture, documentation, and monitoring coverage.
tasks.reading_content_md:
# The Finish Line

Before the 'Big Switch', the lead engineers perform a **PRR**. We check: Is the backup working? Is the auto-scaler tuned? Is the monitoring dashboard accurate? This ensures we don't just launch, we launch with confidence.

tasks.title: PRR Task
tasks.description: Create a 'Launch Readiness' checklist in Markdown.
tasks.coding_starter_code:
# Launch Readiness Checklist
- [ ] Infrastructure as Code applied
- [ ] CI/CD Pipelines green
- [ ] Monitoring active
- [ ] Backups verified

quiz_questions.question: If pods were scaling up and down too frequently (flapping), which setting should be adjusted?

tasks.title: Transformation Complete
tasks.description: Reflecting on 132 days of transformation and celebrating the new automated enterprise.
tasks.reading_content_md:
# Project Gamma: SUCCESS

We have successfully migrated the enterprise to the cloud. We have mastered Linux, AWS, Terraform, Docker, K8s, and Jenkins. The mission of automating and securing the infrastructure is complete. The team is now ready for the next frontier.

tasks.title: Final Milestone Quiz

What is the primary deliverable of the Launch phase?

tasks.reading_content_md:
# The Observability Mindset

Monitoring tells you *if* a system is down; **Observability** tells you *why*. In Project Gamma, we need a unified view of:
1. **Logs**: Detailed records of events (ELK Stack).
2. **Metrics**: Numerical indicators of health (Prometheus).
3. **Traces**: The path a single request takes across all services (Jaeger).

tasks.reading_content_md:
# Transformation Retrospective

Project Gamma has concluded. We moved from 'Click-Ops' to a fully automated CI/CD pipeline and an observable K8s cluster. Key success: 100% of infrastructure is now code in Git. Key challenge: Initial learning curve of Terraform state management.

tasks.title: Retrospective Task
tasks.description: Generate a 'Project Gamma Report' summary in JSON format.

tasks.description: Transitioning the platform to the operations team and outlining the Phase 2 goals (Multi-Cloud & Serverless).

tasks.title: Mastery of the Cloud Engine
tasks.description: Final review and project closure for the Cloud & DevOps Transformation journey.
tasks.reading_content_md:
# Mission Accomplished

You have transformed the enterprise. From the first Linux command on Day 2 to managing a global K8s cluster on Day 132, you have mastered the tools of modern DevOps. The infrastructure is now ready to support millions of users for both Project Solaris and Project Beta.

tasks.title: Final Mastery Quiz
tasks.description: The ultimate final exam for the Project Gamma curriculum.

quiz_questions.question: Which tool was the source of truth for our Infrastructure?
What is the benefit of a Blue-Green deployment?
options: It allows zero-downtime updates and easy rollback
